Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is constituted and operated for purposes exclusively related to journalism,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any business activities it carries on are related to its purposes,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: it has trustees or a board of directors, each of whom deals at arm’s length with each other,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: it is not controlled, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever, by a person or by a group of persons that do not deal with each other at arm’s length,

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: it may not, in a taxation year, receive gifts from any one source that represent more than 20% of its total revenues (including donations) for the taxation year, other than a gift

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: made by way of bequest,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: made within 12 months after the time the organization is first registered, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: approved, on a case-by-case basis, by the Minister, and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: no part of its income is payable to, or otherwise available for the personal benefit of, any proprietor, member, shareholder, director, trustee, settlor or like individual; (organisation journalistique admissible)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(4.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, in the manner described in section 168, revoke the registration of a qualified donee referred to in paragraph (a) or (b.1) of the definition qualified donee in subsection (1) for any reason described in subsection 168(1).
Section 149.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (14):

Every registered journalism organization shall, within six months from the end of each taxation year of the organization without notice or demand, file with the Minister both an information return and a public information return for the year in prescribed form and containing prescribed information including, for the public information return, for each donor whose total gifts to the organization in the year exceed $5,000, the name of the donor and the total amount donated.
Paragraphs 149.1(15)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information contained in a public information return referred to in subsection 149.1(14) or (14.1) shall be communicated or otherwise made available to the public by the Minister in such manner as the Minister deems appropriate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister may make available to the public in any manner that the Minister considers appropriate, in respect of each registered, or previously registered, charity, Canadian amateur athletic association, journalism organization and qualified donee referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition qualified donee in subsection (1),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: its name, address and date of registration,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a registered, or previously registered, charity, Canadian amateur athletic association or journalism organization, its registration number, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the effective date of any revocation, annulment or termination of registration; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(22) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, by registered mail, give notice to a person that the application of the person for registration as a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association, registered journalism organization or qualified donee referred to in subparagraph (a)(i) or (iii) of the definition qualified donee in subsection (1) is refused.
Subsections (1) to (6) come into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraph 152(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of tax, if any, deemed by subsection 120(2) or (2.2), 122.5(3), 122.51(2), 122.7(2) or (3), 122.8(4), 122.9(2), 122.91(1), 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2), 127.1(1), 127.41(3) or 210.2(3) or (4) to be paid on account of the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 152(4.2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: redetermine the amount, if any, deemed by subsection 120(2) or (2.2), 122.5(3), 122.51(2), 122.7(2) or (3), 122.8(4), 122.9(2), 122.91(1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3) or 210.2(3) or (4) to be paid on account of the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year or deemed by subsection 122.61(1) to be an overpayment on account of the taxpayer’s liability under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Section 153 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
For the purposes of this Act, an amount (referred to in this subsection as the “excess amount”) is deemed to not have been deducted or withheld under subsection (1) by a person if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the excess amount was, absent the application of this subsection, deducted or withheld by the person under subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the excess amount is in respect of an excess payment (referred to in this subsection as the “total excess payment”) of an individual’s salary, wages or other remuneration by the person to the individual in a particular year, that was paid as a result of a clerical, administrative or system error;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: before the end of the third year after the calendar year in which the excess amount is deducted or withheld,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person elects in prescribed manner to have this subsection apply in respect of the excess amount, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual has repaid, or made an arrangement to repay, the total excess payment less the excess amount;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an information return correcting for the total excess payment has not been issued by the person to the individual prior to the making of the election in subparagraph (c)(i); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any additional criteria specified by the Minister have been met.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of excess payments of salary, wages or other remuneration made after 2015.
Paragraph 157(3)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 1/12 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2), 127.1(1) or 127.41(3) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 157(3.1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 1/4 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2), 127.1(1) or 127.41(3) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Subsection 163(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c.5):

Paragraph.Label: (c.6)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that would be deemed by subsection 122.91(1) to have been paid on account of the person’s tax payable under this Part for the year if those amounts were calculated by reference to the information provided in the return

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that is deemed by subsection 122.91(1) to be a payment on account of the person’s tax payable under this Part for the taxation year,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 163(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (f), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that would be deemed by subsection 125.6(2) to have been paid for the year by the person if that amount were calculated by reference to the information provided in the return filed for the year pursuant to that subsection

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that is deemed by that subsection to be paid for the year by the person.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Subparagraph 164(1)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: before sending the notice of assessment for the year, where the taxpayer is a qualified corporation (as defined in subsection 125.4(1)), an eligible production corporation (as defined in subsection 125.5(1)) or a qualifying journalism organization (as defined in subsection 125.6(1)) and an amount is deemed under subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3) or 125.6(2) to have been paid on account of its tax payable under this Part for the year, refund all or part of any amount claimed in the return as an overpayment for the year, not exceeding the total of those amounts so deemed to have been paid, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Paragraph 168(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization, fails to file an information return as and when required under this Act or a regulation;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 168(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization, accepts a gift the granting of which was expressly or implicitly conditional on the association or organization making a gift to another person, club, society, association or organization.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 168(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
If the Minister gives notice under subsection (1) to a registered charity, to a registered Canadian amateur athletic association or to a registered journalism organization,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if it has applied to the Minister in writing for the revocation of its registration, the Minister shall, forthwith after the mailing of the notice, publish a copy of the notice in the Canada Gazette, and on that publication of a copy of the notice, the registration is revoked; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 168(2) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the Minister may, after the expiration of 30 days from the day of mailing of the notice, or after the expiration of such extended period from the day of mailing of the notice as the Federal Court of Appeal or a judge of that Court, on application made at any time before the determination of any appeal pursuant to subsection 172(3) from the giving of the notice, may fix or allow, publish a copy of the notice in the Canada Gazette, and on that publication of a copy of the notice, the registration is revoked.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 168(4)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person described in any of subparagraphs (a)(i) to (v) and paragraph (b.1) of the definition qualified donee in subsection 149.1(1), that is or was registered by the Minister as a qualified donee or is an applicant for such registration, it objects to a notice under any of subsections (1) and 149.1(4.3) and (22).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (5) come into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraph 172(3)(a.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: confirms a proposal or decision in respect of which a notice was issued under any of subsections 149.1(4.3) and (22) and 168(1) by the Minister, to a person that is a person described in any of subparagraphs (a)(i) to (v) and paragraph (b.1) of the definition qualified donee in subsection 149.1(1) that is or was registered by the Minister as a qualified donee or is an applicant for such registration, or does not confirm or vacate that proposal or decision within 90 days after service of a notice of objection by the person under subsection 168(4) in respect of that proposal or decision,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2020.
Subsections 188.1(6) to (9) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Every registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association and registered journalism organization that fails to file a return for a taxation year as and when required by subsection 149.1(14) or (14.1) is liable to a penalty equal to $500.

Except where subsection (8) or (9) applies, every registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association and registered journalism organization that issues, in a taxation year, a receipt for a gift otherwise than in accordance with this Act and the regulations is liable for the taxation year to a penalty equal to 5% of the amount reported on the receipt as representing the amount in respect of which a taxpayer may claim a deduction under subsection 110.1(1) or a credit under subsection 118.1(3).

Except where subsection (9) applies, if the Minister has, less than five years before a particular time, assessed a penalty under subsection (7) or this subsection for a taxation year of a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization and, after that assessment and in a subsequent taxation year, it issues, at the particular time, a receipt for a gift otherwise than in accordance with this Act and the regulations, it is liable for the subsequent taxation year to a penalty equal to 10% of the amount reported on the receipt as representing the amount in respect of which a taxpayer may claim a deduction under subsection 110.1(1) or a credit under subsection 118.1(3).

If at any time a person makes or furnishes, participates in the making of or causes another person to make or furnish a statement that the person knows, or would reasonably be expected to know but for circumstances amounting to culpable conduct (as defined in subsection 163.2(1)), is a false statement (as defined in subsection 163.2(1)) on a receipt issued by, on behalf of or in the name of another person for the purposes of subsection 110.1(2) or 118.1(2), the person (or, where the person is an officer, employee, official or agent of a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization, the charity, association or organization) is liable for their taxation year that includes that time to a penalty equal to 125% of the amount reported on the receipt as representing the amount in respect of which a taxpayer may claim a deduction under subsection 110.1(1) or a credit under subsection 118.1(3).
Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2020.
The portion of subsection 188.2(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Minister shall, with an assessment referred to in this subsection, give notice by registered mail to a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization that its authority to issue an official receipt referred to in Part XXXV of the Income Tax Regulations is suspended for one year from the day that is seven days after the day on which the notice is mailed, if the Minister has assessed the charity, association or organization for a taxation year for
Subsection 188.2(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a registered charity, a registered Canadian amateur athletic association or a registered journalism organization fails to report information that is required to be included in a return filed under subsection 149.1(14) or (14.1), the Minister may give notice by registered mail to the charity, association or organization that its authority to issue an official receipt referred to in Part XXXV of the Income Tax Regulations is suspended from the day that is seven days after the day on which the notice is mailed until such time as the Minister notifies the charity, association or organization that the Minister has received the required information in prescribed form.
Subsections (1) and (2) come into force on January 1, 2020.
The portion of subsection 230(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Every qualified donee referred to in paragraphs (a) to (c) of the definition qualified donee in subsection 149.1(1) shall keep records and books of account — in the case of a qualified donee referred to in any of subparagraphs (a)(i) and (iii) and paragraphs (b), (b.1) and (c) of that definition, at an address in Canada recorded with the Minister or designated by the Minister — containing
Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2020.
The portion of subsection 241(3.2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An official may provide to any person the following taxpayer information relating to another person (in this subsection referred to as the “registrant”) that was at any time a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization:
Paragraph 241(3.2)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: financial statements required to be filed with an information return referred to in subsection 149.1(14) or (14.1);

Subsection.Text: Section 241 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3.3):
The Minister may communicate or otherwise make available to the public, in any manner that the Minister considers appropriate, the following taxpayer information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the names of each organization with respect to which an individual can be entitled to a deduction under subsection 118.02(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the start and, if applicable, end of the period in which paragraph (a) applies in respect of any particular organization.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 241(4)(d) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (xvi):

Subparagraph.Label: (xvi.1)
Subparagraph.Text: to a person employed or engaged in the service of an office or agency, of the Government of Canada or of a province, whose mandate includes the provision of assistance (as defined in subsection 125.6(1)) in respect of qualified Canadian journalism organizations, solely for the purpose of the administration or enforcement of the program under which the assistance is offered,

Subparagraph.Label: (xvi.2)
Subparagraph.Text: to a body referred to in paragraph (b) of the definition qualified Canadian journalism organization in subsection 248(1), solely for the purpose of determining eligibility for designation under that paragraph,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force on January 1, 2020.
The definition passenger vehicle in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: passenger vehicle means an automobile

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acquired after June 17, 1987, other than an automobile that is acquired after that date pursuant to an obligation in writing entered into before June 18, 1987 or that is a zero-emission vehicle, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: leased under a lease entered into, extended or renewed after June 17, 1987; (voiture de tourisme)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: qualified Canadian journalism organization, at any time, means a corporation, partnership or trust that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: meets the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: it is incorporated under the laws of Canada or a province,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the chairperson or other presiding officer, and at least 3/4 of the directors or other similar officers, are citizens of Canada, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: it is resident in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a partnership,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: it is formed under the laws of a province, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: individuals who are citizens of Canada or persons, or partnerships, described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iii) hold interests in the partnership

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: representing in value at least 75% of the total value of the partnership property, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: that result in at least 75% of each income or loss of the partnership from any source being included in the determination of their incomes,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a trust,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: it is formed under the laws of a province,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: it is resident in Canada, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: if interests as a beneficiary under the trust are held by one or more persons or partnerships, at least 75% of the fair market value of all interests as a beneficiary under the trust are held by

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: individuals who are citizens of Canada, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: persons or partnerships described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iii),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: it operates in Canada, including that its content is edited, designed and, except in the case of digital content, published in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: it is primarily engaged in the production of original news content, which

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: must be primarily focused on matters of general interest and reports of current events, including coverage of democratic institutions and processes, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: must not be primarily focused on a particular topic such as industry-specific news, sports, recreation, arts, lifestyle or entertainment,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: it regularly employs two or more journalists who deal at arm’s length with the organization in the production of its content,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is not significantly engaged in the production of content

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: to promote the interests, or report on the activities, of an organization, an association or its members,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: for a government, Crown corporation or government agency, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: to promote goods or services, and

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is not a Crown corporation, municipal corporation or government agency, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is designated at that time by the Minister and, for this purpose, the Minister shall take into account any recommendations of a body established for the purpose of this definition; (organisation journalistique canadienne qualifiée)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: registered journalism organization means a qualifying journalism organization (as defined in subsection 149.1(1)) that has applied to the Minister in prescribed form for registration, that has been registered and whose registration has not been revoked; (organisation journalistique enregistrée)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: zero-emission passenger vehicle, of a taxpayer, means an automobile of the taxpayer that is included in Class 54 of Schedule II to the Income Tax Regulations; (voiture de tourisme zéro émission)
zero-emission vehicle, of a taxpayer, means a motor vehicle that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a plug-in hybrid that meets prescribed conditions or is fully

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: electric, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: powered by hydrogen,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is acquired, and becomes available for use, by the taxpayer after March 18, 2019 and before 2028, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not a vehicle

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that has been used, or acquired for use, for any purpose before it was acquired by the taxpayer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the taxpayer has, at any time, made an election under subsection 1103(2j) of the Income Tax Regulations,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: assistance has been paid by the Government of Canada under a prescribed program, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: an amount has been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) by another person or partnership. (véhicule zéro émission)

Subsection.Text: Subsections 248(17) and (17.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Application of subsection (16) to certain vehicles and aircraft
Subsection.Text: If the input tax credit of a taxpayer under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act in respect of a passenger vehicle, zero-emission passenger vehicle or aircraft is determined with reference to subsection 202(4) of that Act, subparagraphs (16)(a)(i) to (iii) are to be read as they apply in respect of the vehicle or aircraft, as the case may be, as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at the beginning of the first taxation year or fiscal period of the taxpayer commencing after the end of the taxation year or fiscal period, as the case may be, in which the goods and services tax in respect of such property was considered for the purposes of determining the input tax credit to be payable, if the tax was considered for the purposes of determining the input tax credit to have become payable in the reporting period, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no such tax was considered for the purposes of determining the input tax credit to have become payable in the reporting period, at the end of the reporting period; or

Subsection.MarginalNote: Application of subsection (16.1) to certain vehicles and aircraft
Subsection.Text: If the input tax refund of a taxpayer under An Act respecting the Québec sales tax, R.S.Q., c. T-0.1, in respect of a passenger vehicle, zero-emission passenger vehicle or aircraft is determined with reference to section 252 of that Act, subparagraphs (16.1)(a)(i) to (iii) are to be read as they apply in respect of the vehicle or aircraft, as the case may be, as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at the beginning of the first taxation year or fiscal period of the taxpayer that begins after the end of the taxation year or fiscal period, as the case may be, in which the Quebec sales tax in respect of such property was considered for the purposes of determining the input tax refund to be payable, if the tax was considered for the purposes of determining the input tax refund to have become payable in the reporting period, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no such tax was considered for the purposes of determining the input tax refund to have become payable in the reporting period, at the end of the reporting period; or

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1), (4) and (5) are deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Subsection (3) comes into force on January 1, 2020.
The portion of subsection 253.1(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of section 149.1 and subsections 188.1(1) and (2), if a registered charity, a registered Canadian amateur athletic association or a registered journalism organization holds an interest as a member of a partnership, the member shall not, solely because of its acquisition and holding of that interest, be considered to carry on any business of the partnership if
Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2020.

Section.Text: The Canada Pension Plan is amended by adding the following after section 21:

Section.MarginalNote: Excess payment — amount deemed not deducted

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), an amount deducted by an employer under subsection 21(1) for a year after 2015 in respect of an excess payment that was paid — as a result of a clerical, administrative or system error — as remuneration to an employee in respect of pensionable employment is deemed not to have been deducted for the purposes of this Act if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before the end of the third year after the calendar year in which the amount was deducted,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the employer elects to have this section apply in respect of the amount, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the employee has repaid, or made an arrangement to repay, the employer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the employer has not filed an information return correcting for the excess payment before making the election referred to in subparagraph (a)(i); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any additional conditions specified by the Minister are met.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the amount that is deemed not to have been deducted is the amount that was deducted by the employer or, if the amount determined by the following formula is less than the amount that was deducted by the employer, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the aggregate of all amounts that were deducted by the employer as the employee’s contributions for the year in question; and
is the aggregate of all amounts that would have been deducted by the employer as the employee’s contributions for that year had the employer not made the excess payment referred to in subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: Section 38 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3.2):

If an amount remitted by an employer is deemed under section 21.01 not to have been deducted, the Minister may refund that amount to the employer if the employer applies to the Minister for the refund within four years after the end of the year for which the amount was remitted.
Section 38 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):
Subsection (7) does not apply in respect of an amount referred to in subsection (3.3) that is refunded or applied under this Act to any other liability.

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application — subsection 114(2) of Canada Pension Plan

Subsection.Text: Subsection 114(2) of the Canada Pension Plan does not apply in respect of the amendments to that Act contained in sections 45 and 46 of this Act.
Sections 45 and 46 come into force, in accordance with subsection 114(4) of the Canada Pension Plan, on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Cultural Property Export and Import Act

Subsection.Text: Subsection 32(1) of the Cultural Property Export and Import Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Request for determination of Review Board

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subparagraph 39(1)(a)(i.1), paragraph 110.1(1)(c), the definition total cultural gifts in subsection 118.1(1) and subsection 118.1(10) of the Income Tax Act, where a person disposes of or proposes to dispose of an object to an institution or a public authority designated under subsection (2), the person, institution or public authority may request, by notice in writing given to the Review Board, a determination by the Review Board as to whether the object meets the criterion set out in paragraph 29(3)(b) and a determination by the Review Board of the fair market value of the object.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
Subsection 33(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Where the Review Board determines or redetermines the fair market value of an object in respect of which a request was made under section 32 and determines that the object meets the criterion set out in paragraph 29(3)(b), it shall, where the object has been irrevocably disposed of to a designated institution or public authority, issue to the person who made the disposition a certificate attesting to the fair market value and to the meeting of that criterion , in such form as the Minister of National Revenue may specify.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.

Section.Text: The Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after section 82:

Section.MarginalNote: Excess payment — amount deemed not deducted

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), an amount deducted by an employer under subsection 82(1) for a year after 2015 in respect of an excess payment that was paid — as a result of a clerical, administrative or system error — as remuneration to an insured person is deemed not to have been deducted for the purposes of this Act if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before the end of the third year after the calendar year in which the amount was deducted,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the employer elects to have this section apply in respect of the amount, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the insured person has repaid, or made an arrangement to repay, the employer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the employer has not filed an information return correcting for the excess payment before making the election referred to in subparagraph (a)(i); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any additional conditions specified by the Minister are met.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the amount that is deemed not to have been deducted is the amount that was deducted by the employer or, if the amount determined by the following formula is less than the amount that was deducted by the employer, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the aggregate of all amounts that were deducted by the employer as the employee’s premiums for the year in question; and
is the aggregate of all amounts that would have been deducted by the employer as the employee’s premiums for that year had the employer not made the excess payment referred to in subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: Section 96 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

If an amount remitted by an employer is deemed under section 82.01 not to have been deducted, the Minister may refund that amount to the employer if the employer applies to the Minister for the refund within three years after the end of the year for which the amount was remitted.
Subsection 96(13.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (13), no interest shall be paid on a refund payable under subsection (3.1), (8.7), (8.91), (8.94), (8.97) or (8.98) or on the amount of a refund payable under any of those subsections that is applied under this Act to any other liability.
Paragraph 1100(1)(a) of the Income Tax Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (xxxviii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (xxxix):

Subparagraph.Label: (xl)
Subparagraph.Text: of Class 54, 30 per cent, and

Subparagraph.Label: (xli)
Subparagraph.Text: of Class 55, 40 per cent,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 1100(1)(b)(i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the capital cost of the property was incurred in the taxation year and after November 12, 1981,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: if the property is an accelerated investment incentive property and the capital cost of the property was incurred before 2024, the lesser of

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: 150 per cent of the amount for the year calculated in accordance with Schedule III, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the amount determined for paragraph 1(b) of Schedule III, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the property is not an accelerated investment incentive property and is not described in any of subparagraphs (b)(iii) to (v) of the description of F in subsection (2), 50 per cent of the amount for the year calculated in accordance with Schedule III, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 1100(1)(c)(i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the aggregate of the amounts for the year obtained by apportioning the capital cost to the taxpayer of each property over the life of the property remaining at the time the cost was incurred, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the property is accelerated investment incentive property, the portion of the amount determined under clause (A) that is in respect of the property multiplied by

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: 0.5, if the property becomes available for use in the year and before 2024, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: 0.25, if the property becomes available for use in the year and after 2023, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 1100(1)(ta)(v) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: property described in any of subparagraphs (b)(iii) to (v) of the description of F in subsection (2),

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 1100(1)(v)(iv) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the capital cost of the property to the taxpayer multiplied by

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: 50 per cent, in the case of an accelerated investment incentive property acquired in the year and before 2024,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: 16 2/3 per cent, in the case of property acquired in the year, other than

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: property described in any of subparagraphs (b)(iii) to (v) of the description of F in subsection (2), and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: 33 1/3 per cent, in any other case, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1100(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

The amount that a taxpayer may deduct for a taxation year under subsection (1) in respect of property of a class in Schedule II is to be determined as if the undepreciated capital cost to the taxpayer at the end of the taxation year (before making any deduction under subsection (1) for the taxation year) of property of the class were adjusted by adding the positive or negative amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is, in respect of property of the class that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year and that is accelerated investment incentive property or property included in Class 54 or 55,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is not included in paragraph (1)(v) or in any of Classes 12, 13, 14, 15, 43.1, 43.2, 53, 54 and 55 or in Class 43 in the circumstances described in paragraph (d),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1/2, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer before 2024, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer after 2023,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the class is Class 43.1,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 2 1/3, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer before 2024,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1 1/2, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2024 or 2025, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 5/6, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer after 2025,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the class is Class 43.2,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer before 2024,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1/2, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2024, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, in any other case,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (d)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is included in Class 53 or — for property acquired after 2025 — is included in Class 43 and would have been included in Class 53 if it had been acquired in 2025,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer before 2024,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1/2, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2024 or 2025, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 5/6, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer after 2025,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (e)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the class is Class 54,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 2 1/3, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer before 2024,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1 1/2, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2024 or 2025, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 5/6, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer after 2025,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (f)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the class is Class 55,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1 1/2, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer before 2024,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 7/8, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2024 or 2025, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 3/8, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer after 2025, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (g)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, nil;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined, in respect of the class, by the formula

is the total of all amounts, if any, each of which is an amount included in the description of A in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) of the Act in respect of property of the class that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year and that is accelerated investment incentive property or property included in Class 54 or 55, as the case may be, and
is the amount, if any, by which the amount determined for G exceeds the amount determined for F in the description of C; and
is the amount determined, in respect of the class, by the formula
is the total of all amounts each of which

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is an amount added to the undepreciated capital cost to the taxpayer of property of the class

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: because of element A in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) of the Act in respect of property (other than accelerated investment incentive property) that was acquired, or became available for use, by the taxpayer in the taxation year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: because of element C or D in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) of the Act in respect of an amount that was repaid in the taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is not in respect of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: property included in paragraph (1)(v), in paragraph (w) of Class 10 or in any of paragraphs (a) to (c), (e) to (i), (k), (l) and (p) to (s) of Class 12,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: property included in any of Classes 13, 14, 15, 23, 24, 27, 29, 34, 52, 54 and 55,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: where the taxpayer was a corporation described in subsection (16) throughout the taxation year, property that was specified leasing property of the taxpayer at that time,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: property that was deemed to have been acquired by the taxpayer in a preceding taxation year by reason of the application of paragraph 16.1(1)(b) of the Act in respect of a lease to which the property was subject immediately before the time at which the taxpayer last acquired the property, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: property considered to have become available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year by reason of paragraph 13(27)(b) or (28)(c) of the Act, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted from the undepreciated capital cost to the taxpayer of property of the class

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: because of element F or G in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) of the Act in respect of property disposed of in the taxation year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: because of element J in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) of the Act in respect of an amount the taxpayer received or was entitled to receive in the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a taxation year begins in 2023 and ends in 2024, the factor determined for A in subsection (2) is to be replaced by the factor determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the factor otherwise determined for A in subsection (2) for 2023,

is the amount that would be determined for D in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2023,
is the factor otherwise determined for A in subsection (2) for 2024, and

is the amount that would be determined for D in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2024; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a taxation year begins in 2025 and ends in 2026, the factor determined for A in subsection (2) is to be replaced by the factor determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the factor otherwise determined for A in subsection (2) for 2025,

is the amount that would be determined for D in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2025,
is the factor otherwise determined for A in subsection (2) for 2026, and

is the amount that would be determined for D in subsection (2) if the only property that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year were property that became available for use by the taxpayer in 2026.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (2), in respect of property of a class in Schedule II that is accelerated investment incentive property of a taxpayer solely because of subparagraph 1104(4)(b)(i),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no amount is to be included in respect of the property in determining the amount for D in subsection (2) in respect of the class to the extent that the amount includes expenditures incurred by any person or partnership before November 21, 2018, unless the person or partnership from which the taxpayer acquired the property dealt at arm’s length with the taxpayer and held the property as inventory; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount excluded from the amount determined for D in subsection (2) in respect of the class because of paragraph (a) is to be included in determining the amount for F in subsection (2) in respect of the class, unless no amount in respect of the property would be so included if the property were not accelerated investment incentive property of the taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 1100(2.2)(h) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: no amount shall be included in determining an amount for F in subsection (2) in respect of the property;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 1100(2.2)(k) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: if the property is a property described in paragraph (1)(v), its subparagraph (iv) shall be read, in respect of the property, as “33 1/3 per cent of the capital cost of the property to the taxpayer, and”.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1100(2.3) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

If a taxpayer has disposed of a property and, because of paragraph (2.2)(h), no amount is required to be included in determining an amount for F in subsection (2) in respect of the property by the person that acquired the property, no amount shall be included by the taxpayer in determining an amount for G in subsection (2) in respect of the disposition of the property.
The portion of subsection 1102(14) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsections (14.11) to (14.13), for the purposes of this Part and Schedule II, if a property is acquired by a taxpayer
Section 1102 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (14.12):
Subsection (14) does not apply to an acquisition of property by a taxpayer from a person in respect of which the property is a zero-emission vehicle included in Class 54 or 55.
Section 1102 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (20):

For the purposes of subsection 1104(4), if, in the absence of this subsection, a taxpayer would be considered to be dealing at arm’s length with another person or partnership as a result of a transaction or series of transactions the principal purpose of which may reasonably be considered to have been to cause one or more properties of the taxpayer to qualify as accelerated investment incentive property, the taxpayer shall be considered not to be dealing at arm’s length with the other person or partnership in respect of the acquisition of those properties.
Section 1102 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (25):
For the purpose of the definition zero-emission vehicle in subsection 248(1) of the Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is a prescribed condition that the motor vehicle has a battery capacity of at least 7 kWh; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the federal purchase incentive announced on March 19, 2019 is a prescribed program.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1), (2) and (4) are deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
Section 1103 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (2i):

Subsection.Label: (2j)
Subsection.Text: A taxpayer may, in its return of income filed with the Minister on or before its filing-due date for the taxation year in which a property is acquired, elect not to include the property in Class 54 or 55 in Schedule II, as the case may be.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
Section 1104 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
For the purposes of this Part and Schedules II to VI, accelerated investment incentive property means property of a taxpayer (other than property included in Class 54 or 55) that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is acquired by the taxpayer after November 20, 2018 and becomes available for use before 2028; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: meets either of the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the property

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: has not been used for any purpose before it was acquired by the taxpayer, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is not a property in respect of which an amount has been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) of the Act by another person or partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the property was not

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: acquired in circumstances where

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the taxpayer was deemed to have been allowed or deducted an amount under paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act in respect of the property in computing income for previous taxation years, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the undepreciated capital cost of depreciable property of a prescribed class of the taxpayer was reduced by an amount determined by reference to the amount by which the capital cost of the property to the taxpayer exceeds its cost amount, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: previously owned or acquired by the taxpayer or by a person or partnership with which the taxpayer did not deal at arm’s length at any time when the property was owned or acquired by the person or partnership.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 1104(17)(a) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: included in Class 43.1 because of its subparagraph (c)(i), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: described in

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: any of subparagraphs (d)(vii) to (ix), (xi), (xiii), (xiv), (xvi) and (xvii) of Class 43.1, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: paragraph (a) of Class 43.2; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies to property acquired after March 21, 2016 that has not been used or acquired for use before March 22, 2016. However, in respect of property acquired before March 22, 2017, clause 1104(17)(a)(ii)(A) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), is to be read as follows:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: any of subparagraphs (d)(viii), (ix), (xi), (xiii), (xiv), (xvi) and (xvii) of Class 43.1, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1106(3) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (d), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the Memorandum of Understanding between the Government of Canada and the Respective Governments of the Flemish, French and German-Speaking Communities of the Kingdom of Belgium concerning Audiovisual Coproduction.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 12, 2018.
The definition registered organization in section 3500 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: registered organization means a registered charity, a registered Canadian amateur athletic association, registered journalism organization or a registered national arts service organization. (organisation enregistrée)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraphs 5800(1)(d) and (e) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any record of the minutes of meetings of the executive of a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any record of the minutes of meetings of the members of a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: all documents and by-laws governing a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization,

ContinuedParagraph.Text: the period ending on the day that is two years after the date on which the registration of the registered charity, the registered Canadian amateur athletic association or the registered journalism organization under the Act is revoked;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of all records and books of account that are not described in paragraph (d) and that relate to a registered charity, registered Canadian amateur athletic association or registered journalism organization whose registration under the Act is revoked, and in respect of the vouchers and accounts necessary to verify the information in such records and books of account, the period ending on the day that is two years after the date on which the registration of the registered charity, the registered Canadian amateur athletic association or the registered journalism organization under the Act is revoked;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on January 1, 2020.
Section 7307 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
For the purposes of paragraph 13(7)(i) of the Act, the amount prescribed in respect of a zero-emission passenger vehicle of a taxpayer is the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the sum that would have been payable in respect of federal and provincial sales taxes on the acquisition of the vehicle if it had been acquired by the taxpayer at a cost equal to A, before the application of the federal and provincial sales taxes.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.

Provision.Label: (I)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(vii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (vii)

Provision.Label: (vii)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(xii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(xiv) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (xiv)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (xv) and “and” at the end of subparagraph (xvi) and by adding the following after subparagraph (xvi):

Provision.Label: (xvii)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, for the purpose of charging electric vehicles, including charging stations, transformers, distribution and control panels, circuit breakers, conduits and related wiring, if

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: the equipment is situated

Provision.Label: (I)
Provision.Text: on the load side of an electricity meter used for billing purposes by a power utility, or

Provision.Label: (II)
Provision.Text: on the generator side of an electricity meter used to measure electricity generated by the taxpayer or the lessee, as the case may be,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: more than 75 per cent of the electrical equipment capacity is dedicated to charging electric vehicles, and

Provision.Label: (C)
Provision.Text: the equipment is

Provision.Label: (I)
Provision.Text: an electric vehicle charging station (other than a building) that supplies more than 10 kilowatts of continuous power, or

Provision.Label: (II)
Provision.Text: used primarily in connection with one or more electric vehicle charging stations (other than buildings) each of which supplies more than 10 kilowatts of continuous power, or

Provision.Label: (xviii)
Provision.Text: fixed location energy storage property that

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of storing electrical energy

Provision.Label: (I)
Provision.Text: including batteries, compressed air energy storage, flywheels, ancillary equipment (including control and conditioning equipment) and related structures, and

Provision.Label: (II)
Provision.Text: not including buildings, pumped hydroelectric storage, hydro electric dams and reservoirs, property used solely for backup electrical energy, batteries used in motor vehicles, fuel cell systems where the hydrogen is produced via steam reformation of methane and property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: either

Provision.Label: (I)
Provision.Text: if the electrical energy to be stored is used in connection with property of the taxpayer or a lessee of the taxpayer, as the case may be, is described in paragraph (c) or would be described in this paragraph if it were read without reference to this subparagraph, or

Provision.Label: (II)
Provision.Text: meets the condition that the efficiency of the electrical energy storage system that includes the property – computed by reference to the quantity of electrical energy supplied to and discharged from the electrical energy storage system – is greater than 50%, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) and (6) to (8) apply to property acquired after March 21, 2016 that has not been used or acquired for use before March 22, 2016.
Subsection (5) applies in respect of property acquired for use after March 21, 2017 that has not been used or acquired for use before March 22, 2017.

and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to property acquired after March 21, 2016 that has not been used or acquired for use before March 22, 2016.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Section.Text: Section 2 of Schedule V to the Regulations is replaced by the following:
If the taxpayer has not been granted an allowance in respect of the mine or right for a previous taxation year, the rate for a taxation year is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1.5, if the property is an accelerated investment incentive property acquired before 2024,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1.25, if the property is an accelerated investment incentive property acquired after 2023, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1, in any other case;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the capital cost of the mine or right to the taxpayer;
is the residual value, if any, of the mine or right; and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer has acquired a right to remove only a specified number of units, the specified number of units of material that the taxpayer acquired a right to remove, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the number of units of commercially mineable material estimated as being in the mine when the mine or right was acquired.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (b) does not apply,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if section 2 applied in the previous year to determine the rate employed to determine the allowance for the year, the rate that would have been determined under section 2 if paragraph (c) of the description of A in that section applied, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, the rate employed to determine the allowance for the most recent year for which an allowance was granted; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 3(b) of Schedule V to the Regulations before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where it has been established that the number of units of material remaining to be mined in the previous taxation year was in fact different from the quantity that was employed in determining the rate for the previous year referred to in paragraph (a), or where it has been established that the capital cost of the mine or right is substantially different from the amount that was employed in determining the rate for that previous year, a rate determined by dividing the amount that would be the undepreciated capital cost to the taxpayer of the mine or right as of the commencement of the year if paragraph (c) of the description of A in section 2 had applied in respect of each previous taxation year minus the residual value, if any, by

Section.Text: Section 2 of Schedule VI to the Regulations is replaced by the following:
If the taxpayer has not been granted an allowance in respect of the limit or right for a previous taxation year, the rate for a taxation year is an amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1.5, if the property is an accelerated investment incentive property acquired before 2024,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1.25, if the property is an accelerated investment incentive property acquired after 2023, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1, in any other case;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the capital cost of the mine or right to the taxpayer;
is the residual value of the timber limit;

is the total of all amounts expended by the taxpayer after the commencement of the taxpayer’s 1949 taxation year that are included in the capital cost to the taxpayer of the timber limit or right, for surveys, cruises or preparation of prints, maps or plans for the purpose of obtaining a licence or right to cut timber; and
is the quantity of timber in the limit or the quantity of timber the taxpayer has obtained a right to cut, as the case may be, (expressed in cords, board feet or cubic metres) as shown by a cruise.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(a) of Schedule VI to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (b) does not apply,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if section 2 applied in the previous year to determine the rate employed to determine the allowance for the year, the rate that would have been determined under section 2 if paragraph (c) of the description of A in that section applied, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, the rate employed to determine the allowance for the most recent year for which an allowance was granted; and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 3(b)(i) of Schedule VI to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that would be the undepreciated capital cost to the taxpayer of the limit or right as of the commencement of the year if paragraph (c) of the description of A in section 2 had applied in respect of each previous taxation year, minus the residual value,

Subsection.Text: The definition passenger vehicle in subsection 123(1) of the Excise Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: passenger vehicle means a passenger vehicle or a zero-emission passenger vehicle, as those terms are defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act; (voiture de tourisme)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
The portion of the description of A in paragraph 201(b) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: représente la taxe qui serait payable par lui relativement à la voiture s’il l’avait acquise à l’endroit ci-après au moment donné pour une contrepartie égale au montant qui serait, selon celui des alinéas 13(7)g) à i) de la Loi de l’impôt sur le revenu qui est applicable relativement à la voiture, réputé être, pour l’application de l’article 13 de cette loi, le coût en capital pour un contribuable d’une voiture de tourisme à laquelle l’alinéa en cause s’applique s’il n’était pas tenu compte de l’élément B des formules figurant à l’alinéa 7307(1)b) et au paragraphe 7307(1.1) du Règlement de l’impôt sur le revenu :

Subsection.Text: The portion of the description of A in paragraph 201(b) of the English version of the Act after subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSubclause.Text: for consideration equal to the amount that would, under whichever of paragraphs 13(7)(g) to (i) of the Income Tax Act is applicable in respect of the vehicle, be deemed to be, for the purposes of section 13 of that Act, the capital cost to a taxpayer of a passenger vehicle in respect of which that paragraph applies if the formulae in paragraph 7307(1)(b) and subsection 7307(1.1) of the Income Tax Regulations were read without reference to the description of B,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to any passenger vehicle that is acquired, imported or brought into a participating province after March 18, 2019.
Subsection 202(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the consideration paid or payable by a registrant for an improvement to a passenger vehicle of the registrant increases the cost to the registrant of the vehicle to an amount that exceeds the amount that would, under whichever of paragraphs 13(7)(g) to (i) of the Income Tax Act is applicable in respect of the vehicle, be deemed to be, for the purposes of section 13 of that Act, the capital cost to a taxpayer of a passenger vehicle in respect of which that paragraph applies if the formulae in paragraph 7307(1)(b) and subsection 7307(1.1) of the Income Tax Regulations were read without reference to the description of B, the tax calculated on that excess shall not be included in determining an input tax credit of the registrant for any reporting period of the registrant.
Subsection (1) applies to any improvement to a passenger vehicle that is acquired, imported or brought into a participating province after March 18, 2019.
Subparagraph (b)(ii) of the definition imported taxable supply in section 217 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the recipient is not acquiring the property for consumption, use or supply exclusively in the course of its commercial activities or the property is a passenger vehicle that the recipient is acquiring for use in Canada as capital property in its commercial activities and that has a capital cost to the recipient exceeding the amount deemed under any of paragraphs 13(7)(g) to (i) of the Income Tax Act to be the capital cost of the vehicle to the recipient for the purposes of section 13 of that Act;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b.01)(ii) of the definition imported taxable supply in section 217 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the recipient is not acquiring the property for consumption, use or supply exclusively in the course of its commercial activities or the property is a passenger vehicle that the recipient is acquiring for use in Canada as capital property in its commercial activities and that has a capital cost to the recipient exceeding the amount deemed under any of paragraphs 13(7)(g) to (i) of the Income Tax Act to be the capital cost of the vehicle to the recipient for the purposes of section 13 of that Act;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b.1)(ii) of the definition imported taxable supply in section 217 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the recipient is not acquiring, as the recipient of the taxable supply, the property for consumption, use or supply exclusively in the course of its commercial activities or the property is a passenger vehicle that the recipient is acquiring for use in Canada as capital property in its commercial activities and that has a capital cost to the recipient exceeding the amount deemed under any of paragraphs 13(7)(g) to (i) of the Income Tax Act to be the capital cost of the vehicle to the recipient for the purposes of section 13 of that Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) apply in respect of supplies made after March 18, 2019.
The portion of subsection 235(1) of the French version of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Taxe nette en cas de location de voiture de tourisme

Subsection.Text: Lorsque la taxe relative aux fournitures d’une voiture de tourisme, effectuées aux termes d’un bail, devient payable par un inscrit, ou est payée par lui sans être devenue payable, au cours de son année d’imposition, et que le total de la contrepartie des fournitures qui serait déductible dans le calcul du revenu de l’inscrit pour l’année pour l’application de la Loi de l’impôt sur le revenu s’il était un contribuable aux termes de cette loi et s’il n’était pas tenu compte de l’article 67.3 de cette loi, excède le montant, relatif à cette contrepartie, qui serait déductible dans le calcul du revenu de l’inscrit pour l’année pour l’application de cette loi s’il était un contribuable aux termes de cette loi et s’il n’était pas tenu compte de l’élément B des formules figurant à l’alinéa 7307(1)b), au paragraphe 7307(1.1) et à l’alinéa 7307(3)b) du Règlement de l’impôt sur le revenu, le montant obtenu par la formule ci-après est ajouté dans le calcul de la taxe nette de l’inscrit pour la période de déclaration indiquée :
Paragraph 235(1)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount in respect of that consideration that would be deductible in computing the registrant’s income for the year for the purposes of the Income Tax Act, if the registrant were a taxpayer under that Act and the formulae in paragraph 7307(1)(b), subsection 7307(1.1) and paragraph 7307(3)(b) of the Income Tax Regulations were read without reference to the description of B,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
Part II of Schedule V to the Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.3:

Provision.Text: A supply of a service if all or substantially all of the consideration for the supply is reasonably attributable to two or more particular services, each of which meets the following conditions:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the particular service is rendered in the course of making the supply; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: a supply of the particular service would be a supply included in any of sections 5 to 7.3, if the particular service were supplied separately.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any supply made after March 19, 2019.
Part I of Schedule VI to the Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:

Provision.Text: A supply of an ovum, as defined in section 3 of the Assisted Human Reproduction Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 20, 2019.

Provision.Label: (i)

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: a registered nurse.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any supply made after March 19, 2019.

Section.Text: In vitro embryos, as defined in section 3 of the Assisted Human Reproduction Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 20, 2019.
Part I of Schedule X to the Act is amended by adding the following after section 26:

Provision.Text: In vitro embryos, as defined in section 3 of the Assisted Human Reproduction Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 20, 2019.

For the purposes of this Part, if any of paragraphs 13(7)(g) to (i) of the Income Tax Act deems an amount to be the capital cost to a registrant of a passenger vehicle for the purposes of section 13 of that Act, the amount, if any, by which
The description of B in paragraph 21.3(4)(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount deemed by any of paragraphs 13(7)(g) to (i) of the Income Tax Act to be the capital cost to the registrant of the vehicle for the purposes of section 13 of that Act,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
The description of B in paragraph (a) of the definition dutiable amount in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001 is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the percentage set out in paragraph 2(a) of Schedule 7, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition low-THC cannabis product in section 2 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: consisting entirely of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: fresh cannabis,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: dried cannabis, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: oil that contains anything referred to in item 1 or 3 of Schedule 1 to the Cannabis Act and that is in liquid form at a temperature of 22 ± 2°C; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any part of which does not have a maximum yield of more than 0.3% THC w/w, taking into account the potential to convert THCA into THC, as determined in accordance with the Cannabis Act. (produit du cannabis à faible teneur en THC)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: dried cannabis has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Cannabis Act. (cannabis séché)
fresh cannabis has the same meaning as in subsection 1(1) of the Cannabis Regulations. (cannabis frais)
THCA means delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinolic acid. (ATHC)

total THC of a cannabis product means the total quantity of THC, in milligrams, that the cannabis product could yield, taking into account the potential to convert THCA into THC, as determined in accordance with the Cannabis Act. (THC total)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) come into force, or are deemed to have come into force, on May 1, 2019.
Section 172 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: For greater certainty, if an amendment to this Act, or an amendment or enactment that relates to this Act, comes into force on, or applies as of, a particular day that is before the day on which the amendment or enactment is assented to or promulgated, the provisions of this Act and of the Customs Act, as the case may be, that relate to the calculation and payment of interest apply in respect of the amendment or enactment as though it had been assented to or promulgated on the particular day.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on May 1, 2019.
Paragraph (b) of the description of A in section 233.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount obtained by multiplying the fair market value, at the time the contravention occurred, of the cannabis products to which the contravention relates by the percentage set out in paragraph 4(a) of Schedule 7, as that paragraph read at that time;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on May 1, 2019.
Paragraph (b) of the description of A in section 234.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount obtained by multiplying the fair market value, at the time the contravention occurred, of the cannabis products to which the contravention relates by the percentage set out in paragraph 4(a) of Schedule 7, as that paragraph read at that time;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on May 1, 2019.
Subparagraphs 238.1(2)(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the dollar amount set out in subparagraph 1(a)(i) of Schedule 7,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the stamp is in respect of a specified province, three times the dollar amount set out in subparagraph 1(a)(i) of Schedule 7, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on May 1, 2019.
Sections 1 to 4 of Schedule 7 to the Act are replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Any cannabis product produced in Canada or imported: the amount equal to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: in the case of dried cannabis, fresh cannabis, cannabis plants and cannabis plant seeds, the total of

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: $0.25 per gram of flowering material included in the cannabis product or used in the production of the cannabis product,

Provision.Label: (ii)
