Subparagraph.Text: subsection 58(7),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 59(5), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 71(2);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the First Nations Oil and Gas and Moneys Management Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the First Nations Commercial and Industrial Development Act;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 161(1) of the Keeping Canada’s Economy and Jobs Growing Act, as amended by section 233 of the Economic Action Plan 2013 Act, No. 1;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the Family Homes on Reserves and Matrimonial Interests or Rights Act;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the Safe Drinking Water for First Nations Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in section 2 of the First Nations Elections Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Other reference to Minister of Indian Affairs and Northern Development
Subsection.Text: Unless the context requires otherwise, the reference to the “Minister of Indian Affairs and Northern Development” is to be read as a reference to the “Minister of Indigenous Services” in the portion of subsection 22(1) of the Kanesatake Interim Land Base Governance Act before paragraph (a).

Unless the context requires otherwise, the reference to the “Minister” is to be read as a reference to the “Minister of Indigenous Services” in subsection 11(2) of the Saskatchewan Treaty Land Entitlement Act.
In this section, other Act means An Act to amend the Indian Oil and Gas Act, chapter 7 of the Statutes of Canada, 2009.
If section 1 of the other Act comes into force before paragraph 375(1)(b) of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that paragraph 375(1)(b) is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the Indian Oil and Gas Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Minister means the Minister of Indigenous Services. (ministre)

Subsection.Text: If paragraph 375(1)(b) of this Act comes into force before section 1 of the other Act, then, on the day on which that section 1 comes into force, the definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the Indian Oil and Gas Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Minister means the Minister of Indigenous Services. (ministre)

Subsection.Text: If paragraph 375(1)(b) of this Act comes into force on the same day as section 1 of the other Act, then that section 1 is deemed to have come into force before that paragraph 375(1)(b) and subsection (2) applies as a consequence.
On the first day on which both section 3 of the other Act and paragraph 372(1)(b) of this Act are in force, section 15 of the Indian Oil and Gas Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Without derogating from paragraph 24(2)(d) of the Interpretation Act in relation to any other power of the Minister under this Act, the Minister may delegate in writing any of the Minister’s powers under subsections 8(1), 10(1) and (3) and 14(1) to any person employed in the Department of Indigenous Services.

On the first day on which both section 3 of the Addition of Lands to Reserves and Reserve Creation Act (in this section referred to as the “other Act”) and section 3 of the Department of Crown-Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Act, as enacted by section 337 of this Act, are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in section 2 of the other Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Minister means the Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations. (ministre)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 3 of the other Act and the heading before it are repealed.

Section.Text: If Bill C-92, introduced in the 1st session of the 42nd Parliament and entitled An Act respecting First Nations, Inuit and Métis children, youth and families (in this section referred to as the “other Act”), receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 6 of that Act and section 3 of the Department of Indigenous Services Act, as enacted by section 336 of this Act, are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in section 1 of the other Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Minister means the Minister of Indigenous Services. (ministre)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 6 of the other Act and the heading before it are repealed.

Section.Text: If Bill C-94, introduced in the 1st session of the 42nd Parliament and entitled An Act respecting certain payments to be made out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund (in this section referred to as the “other Act”), receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 1 of that Act and section 3 of the Department of Indigenous Services Act, as enacted by section 336 of this Act, are in force, section 1 of the other Act is replaced by the following:

Despite section 161 of the Keeping Canada’s Economy and Jobs Growing Act, as amended by section 233 of the Economic Action Plan 2013 Act, No. 1, there may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities or the Minister of Indigenous Services, in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Treasury Board, in addition to the sum referred to in that section 161, a sum not exceeding $2,200,000,000 to provinces, territories, municipalities, municipal associations, provincial, territorial and municipal entities and First Nations for the purpose of municipal, regional and First Nations infrastructure.

If Bill C-262, introduced in the 1st session of the 42nd Parliament and entitled the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act (in this section referred to as the “other Act”), receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 6 of that Act and section 3 of the Department of Crown-Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Act, as enacted by section 337 of this Act, are in force, section 6 of the other Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations must, within 60 days after the first day of April of every year including and occurring between the years 2017 and 2037, submit a report to each House of Parliament on the implementation of the measures referred to in section 4 and the plan referred to in section 5 for the relevant period.

On the first day on which both section 130 of this Act and section 3 of the Department of Indigenous Services Act, as enacted by section 336 of this Act, are in force, that section 130 is replaced by the following:

Despite section 161 of the Keeping Canada’s Economy and Jobs Growing Act, as amended by section 233 of the Economic Action Plan 2013 Act, No. 1, there may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities or the Minister of Indigenous Services, in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Treasury Board, in addition to the sum referred to in that section 161, a sum not exceeding $2,200,000,000 to provinces, territories, municipalities, municipal associations, provincial, territorial and municipal entities and First Nations for the purpose of municipal, regional and First Nations infrastructure.
The Department of Indian Affairs and Northern Development Act, chapter I-6 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1985, is repealed.

Subsection.Text: This Subdivision, except for subsections 368(2) and 369(2) and sections 376 to 381, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but no later than July 15, 2019.

Subsections 368(2) and 369(2) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that day must not be earlier than the day on which subsections 368(1) and 369(1) come into force.

Section.Text: Section 44 of the First Nations Land Management Act is replaced by the following:
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply in respect of a land code, First Nation laws or an order made under subsection 25.1(1).

TitleText.XRefExternal: First Nations Oil and Gas and Moneys Management Act

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 22(1) of the First Nations Oil and Gas and Moneys Management Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Minister may, by order, add a first nation’s name to Schedule 1 following
Subsection 22(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
On receipt of a resolution of a first nation’s council advising of a change in the name of the first nation, the Minister may, by order, amend Schedule 1 accordingly.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Addition of Lands to Reserves and Reserve Creation Act

Section.Text: On the first day on which both section 675 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2 and section 384 of this Act are in force, section 4 of the Addition of Lands to Reserves and Reserve Creation Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply in respect of an order made under subsection (1).

TitleText.XRefExternal: Federal Prompt Payment for Construction Work Act

Section.Text: The Federal Prompt Payment for Construction Work Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish a regime for prompt payment for construction work performed for the purposes of a construction project in respect of federal real property or federal immovables

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Prompt Payment for Construction Work Act.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: adjudicator means an individual designated as an adjudicator by the Adjudicator Authority. (intervenant expert)
Adjudicator Authority means the entity, designated under section 15, responsible for the designation of adjudicators. (autorité des intervenants experts)

capital repair means any repair intended to extend the normal useful life or to improve the value or productivity of any real property or immovable, but does not include maintenance or repair work performed to prevent the normal deterioration or to maintain the normal, functional state of the real property or immovable. (réparation majeure)
construction project means one or more of the following elements required by a contract between a contractor and Her Majesty or a service provider:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the addition, alteration or capital repair to, or restoration of, any federal real property or federal immovable;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the construction, erection or installation on any federal real property or federal immovable, including the installation of equipment that is essential to the normal or intended use of the federal real property or federal immovable; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the complete or partial demolition or removal of any federal real property or federal immovable. (projet de construction)

Definition.Text: construction work means the supply of materials or services, including the rental of equipment, for the purposes of a construction project located in Canada. (travaux de construction)

contractor means a party to a contract with Her Majesty or a service provider under which that party is to perform construction work, but does not include such a party if they are the lessor or lessee of the real property or immovable to which the construction work relates. (entrepreneur)
federal immovable has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Federal Real Property and Federal Immovables Act. (immeuble fédéral)
federal real property has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Federal Real Property and Federal Immovables Act. (bien réel fédéral)
Her Majesty means Her Majesty in right of Canada. (Sa Majesté)
Minister means the member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada who is designated as the Minister under section 3. (ministre)
notice of adjudication means a written notice provided under subsection 16(2). (avis de renvoi)
notice of non-payment means a written notice provided under subsection 9(3), 10(3) or 11(3). (avis de non-paiement)

proper invoice means an invoice submitted under subsection 9(1) that meets the requirements under this Act as well as any other requirements set out in the contract between the contractor and Her Majesty or a service provider that do not conflict with the requirements under this Act. (facture en règle)

service provider means a party to a contract with Her Majesty under which that party is to provide Her Majesty with services related to federal real property or a federal immovable and may, for the purposes of fulfilling its obligations under that contract, enter into a contract with a person for the carrying out of a construction project, but does not include a party to such a contract if they are the lessor or lessee of the federal real property or federal immovable. (fournisseur de services)
subcontractor means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a party to a contract with a contractor under which that party is to perform construction work; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a party to a contract with any person — other than Her Majesty, a service provider or the contractor — under which that party is to perform construction work for the purposes of the same construction project for which the construction work referred to in paragraph (a) is to be performed. (sous-traitant)

Subsection.Text: The references to a contract in the definitions contractor, service provider and subcontractor in subsection (1) do not include a contract under which a party is to perform construction work as an employee.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, designate a member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada as the Minister for the purpose of this Act.

The purpose of this Act is to promote the orderly and timely carrying out of construction projects in respect of any federal real property or federal immovable by addressing the non-payment of contractors and subcontractors who perform construction work for the purposes of those projects.

This Act applies to Her Majesty, to any service provider and to any contractor or subcontractor that is to perform construction work for the purposes of a construction project located in Canada in respect of any federal real property or federal immovable.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, designate any province if the Governor in Council is, taking into account any criteria set out in the regulations, of the opinion that the province has, by or under the laws of that province, enacted

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a regime for the payment of contractors and subcontractors that is reasonably similar to the one set out in this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in case of non-payment of contractors or subcontractors, an adjudication regime that is reasonably similar to the one set out in this Act.

Subsection.Text: If the Governor in Council designates a province under subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 8(2) and 9(5), sections 10 to 14 and subsection 16(5) no longer apply to any contractor that is to perform construction work for the purposes of a construction project located in that province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act no longer apply to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any subcontractor that is to perform construction work for the purposes of that construction project, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any service provider that is to pay for construction work performed for the purposes of that construction project; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Governor in Council may, with respect to all construction projects located in that province, adapt any provisions of this Act that apply to Her Majesty or continue to apply to contractors for the purpose of addressing any inconsistency or conflict between those provisions and the provincial law.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) does not apply to a construction project if any part of that project straddles the border between two or more provinces.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, exempt any construction project from the application of this Act.

Subsection.Text: Before Her Majesty or a service provider enters into a contract with a person under which that person is to perform construction work, Her Majesty or that service provider must inform the person that they will be subject to this Act if they enter into the contract and provide them with any information prescribed by regulation.

Before a contractor or subcontractor enters into a contract with a person, under which that person is to perform construction work for the purposes of the same construction project for which the construction work referred to in subsection (1) is to be performed, that contractor or subcontractor must inform the person that they will be subject to this Act if they enter into the contract and provide them with any information prescribed by regulation.

Heading.TitleText: Her Majesty or Service Provider to Contractor

Subsection.Text: A contractor must, on a monthly basis or as specified by its contract, submit a proper invoice in writing to Her Majesty or a service provider, as the case may be, with respect to any construction work that was performed by the contractor, or performed and invoiced by any subcontractor in the subcontracting chain, up to the date of the proper invoice and not yet paid for by Her Majesty or that service provider.

Her Majesty or the service provider must pay the contractor for all of the construction work referred to in subsection (1) no later than the 28th day after the day on which the proper invoice is received.

Despite subsection (2), Her Majesty or the service provider may decline to pay for some or all of the construction work referred to in subsection (1) if, no later than the 21st day after the day on which the proper invoice is received, Her Majesty or the service provider provides the contractor with a notice of non-payment.
A proper invoice is not subject to any requirement respecting prior verification of the construction work.

On request, a contractor must inform any subcontractor in the subcontracting chain of the date on which Her Majesty or the service provider received a proper invoice from the contractor under subsection (1).

A contractor that is paid by Her Majesty or a service provider under subsection 9(2) must, no later than the 35th day after the day on which the proper invoice is received by Her Majesty or a service provider, pay each of its subcontractors for the construction work that they invoiced and that was covered by the proper invoice and paid for by Her Majesty or the service provider.
For the purposes of subsection (1), if the contractor is only paid in part, it must distribute the amount received from Her Majesty or the service provider in the following manner:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on a rateable basis, the contractor must pay any of its subcontractors whose construction work is not covered by a notice of non-payment provided under subsection 9(3) and may retain any amount owed to the contractor by Her Majesty or the service provider for any of the contractor’s own construction work that is not covered by such a notice; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: then, from any remaining amount and on a rateable basis, the contractor must pay any of its subcontractors whose work is partly covered by a notice of non-payment provided under subsection 9(3) and may retain any amount owed to the contractor by Her Majesty or the service provider for any of the contractor’s own construction work that is partly covered by such a notice.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), the contractor may decline to pay for some or all of the construction work if, no later than the 28th day after the day on which the proper invoice is received, the contractor provides the subcontractor with a notice of non-payment.

A subcontractor that is paid by a contractor under subsection 10(1) must, no later than the 42nd day after the day on which the proper invoice is received by Her Majesty or the service provider, pay each of its subcontractors for the construction work that they invoiced and that was covered by the proper invoice and paid for by Her Majesty or the service provider.
For the purpose of subsection (1), if the subcontractor is only paid in part, it must distribute the amount received from the contractor in the following manner:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on a rateable basis, the subcontractor must pay any of its subcontractors whose construction work is not covered by a notice of non-payment provided under subsection 10(3) and may retain any amount owed to the subcontractor by the contractor for any of the subcontractor’s own construction work that is not covered by such a notice; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: then, from any remaining amount and on a rateable basis, the subcontractor must pay any of its subcontractors whose work is partly covered by the notice of non-payment provided under subsection 10(3) and may retain any amount owed to the subcontractor by the contractor for any of the subcontractor’s own construction work that is partly covered by such a notice.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), the subcontractor may decline to pay for some or all of the construction work if, no later than the 35th day after the day on which the proper invoice is received, the subcontractor provides its subcontractor with a notice of non-payment.
This section applies to any other subcontractor in the subcontracting chain, with any necessary modifications, so that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any subcontractor that is paid under subsection (1) must pay its subcontractors no later than the 49th day after the day on which the proper invoice is received by Her Majesty or the service provider and those subcontractors must pay any of their subcontractors no later than the 56th day after the day on which the proper invoice is received and so on, in increments of seven days, until the end of the subcontracting chain; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any subcontractor that is paid under subsection (1) may decline to pay any of its subcontractors if it provides them with a notice of non-payment no later than the 42nd day after the day on which the proper invoice is received by Her Majesty or the service provider and those subcontractors may decline to pay any of their subcontractors if they provide them with a notice of non-payment no later than the 49th day after the day on which the proper invoice is received and so on, in increments of seven days, until the end of the subcontracting chain.

Subsection.Text: Any payment for construction work made to a party under subsection 9(2), 10(1) or 11(1) may be subject to a holdback provided for by the contract entered into between that party and the party that is to pay them.

However, the amount of a holdback made under the contract cannot exceed what that amount would be if the holdback was made under the construction law of the province in which the construction project is located.
The amount of the holdback must be paid by no later than the day on which that payment would be made under the construction law of the province in which the construction project is located.

Section.Text: A notice of non-payment must include the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the construction work covered by the notice of non-payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that will not be paid;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons for the non-payment, including whether the party that must pay does not have the necessary funds to do so as a result of also receiving a notice of non-payment that covers the construction work referred to in paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other information prescribed by regulation.

Subsection.Text: Interest must be paid and begins to accrue on the following unpaid amounts from the expiry of the time limit for payment provided under this Act or, when applicable, the shorter time limit set out in the contract between the parties:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is not covered by a notice of non-payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is covered by a notice of non-payment, if the only reason for non-payment of that amount is that the party that must pay does not have the necessary funds to do so as a result of also receiving a notice of non-payment that covers the construction work that must be paid for; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is covered by a notice of non-payment, if there is a reason for non-payment of that amount other than the reason referred to in paragraph (b), but only for the portion of that amount that an adjudicator orders to be paid.

Subsection.Text: The interest rate applicable to the amounts referred to in subsection (1) is the rate prescribed by regulation or, if the contract between the parties sets out an interest rate, the greater of the rate prescribed by regulation and the rate set out in the contract.

Section.Text: The Minister may, in accordance with any criteria prescribed by regulations, designate the Adjudicator Authority.

Subsection.Text: If a contractor or subcontractor has not been fully paid for its construction work within the time limit for payment provided under this Act or, when applicable, the shorter time limit set out in its contract, that contractor or subcontractor may obtain from an adjudicator a determination respecting any dispute over the non-payment by the party that is to pay it under the contract.

A contractor or subcontractor that seeks to have the dispute referred to in subsection (1) determined by an adjudicator must provide the party that is to pay it under the contract with a notice of adjudication no later than the 21st day after the later of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which the contractor receives a certificate of completion with respect to the construction project from Her Majesty or a service provider; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if any of its construction work is covered by the last proper invoice submitted with respect to the construction project, the expiry of the time limit provided under this Act for payment for that work.

Subsection.Text: A notice of adjudication must include the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the names of the parties to the dispute;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a brief description of the dispute, including details of how and when it arose;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount requested to be paid;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the name of a proposed adjudicator; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any other information prescribed by regulation.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (3)(d), the name must be selected from a list of adjudicators established by the Adjudicator Authority and that selection cannot be restricted by contract.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Right to information — certificate of completion
Subsection.Text: On request, a contractor must inform any subcontractor in the subcontracting chain of the date on which the contractor received a certificate of completion with respect to the construction project from Her Majesty or the service provider.
The parties to a dispute must jointly appoint an adjudicator to determine the dispute.
If the parties are unable to jointly appoint an adjudicator, either party may request that the Adjudicator Authority appoint an adjudicator for them.
On request, the Adjudicator Authority must appoint an adjudicator from a list of adjudicators it has established.
The appointment of an adjudicator is subject to that adjudicator’s consent.
An adjudicator must, except in the cases provided for in the regulations, determine the dispute.
The adjudicator’s determination is binding on the parties to the dispute unless they come to a written agreement or the determination is set aside by a court order or arbitral award.
Nothing in this Act restricts the authority of a court or of an arbitrator to consider the merits of a matter determined by an adjudicator.

If a party that is to pay for construction work under its contract is ordered by the adjudicator to make a payment, the party must make the payment no later than the 10th day after the day on which it received the adjudicator’s determination, or within any other time limit set out in the determination.
If the party that is ordered to make the payment fails to do so within the applicable time limit, the party that is to be paid may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspend further construction work without this constituting a breach of the terms of its contract; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: file in the Federal Court or a superior court of a province, as the case may be, a certified copy of the adjudicator’s determination within two years after the day on which that determination was received.

Subsection.Text: On the certified copy being filed, the determination becomes and may be enforced as an order of the Federal Court or superior court of a province, as the case may be.
Subject to subsection (2), each party to the adjudication must pay its own costs and an equal portion of the adjudicator’s fees and expenses.

If the adjudicator is of the opinion that a party has acted in an abusive, scandalous or vexatious manner or in bad faith, the adjudicator may order that party to pay some or all of the other party’s costs or some or all of the other party’s portion of the adjudicator’s fees and expenses.

Section.Text: The adjudicator is not to be required to produce or give evidence in respect of any matter that comes to their knowledge as a result of exercising their powers or performing their duties or functions under this Act.
The Minister may make regulations prescribing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information referred to in section 8 and the form and manner in which it is to be submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the form and content of a proper invoice and the manner in which it is to be submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the form and any additional content of a notice of non-payment and the manner in which it is to be submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the powers, duties and functions of the Adjudicator Authority;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the eligibility and qualifications of individuals who may be designated as adjudicators;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the powers, duties and functions of adjudicators;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the maximum fees for the services of an adjudicator;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the form and any additional content of a notice of adjudication and the manner in which it is to be submitted; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the procedure and time limits governing the adjudication.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations prescribing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: criteria for the purpose of subsection 6(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of any province, any days that are to be excluded from the computation of time for the purposes of the time limits referred to in sections 9 to 11.

Section.Text: Section 126 of the Criminal Code does not apply to or in respect of any contravention or alleged contravention of any provision of this Act or its regulations.
For one year after the day on which this Act comes into force, this Act does not apply in respect of the following contracts to perform construction work:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a contract entered into by a contractor, before the day on which this Act comes into force, with Her Majesty or a service provider; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a contract entered into by a subcontractor, before the day on which this Act comes into force or within one year after that day, with a contractor referred to in paragraph (a) or with any other subcontractor.

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
An offence under any of the following provisions of the Criminal Code:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 57(1) (forgery of or uttering forged passport);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 74(1) (piracy by law of nations);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 75 (piratical acts);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.02 (providing or collecting property for certain activities);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.03 (providing, making available, etc., property or services for terrorist purposes);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.04 (using or possessing property for terrorist purposes);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.12 (freezing of property, disclosure or audit);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 83.18(1) (participation in activity of terrorist group);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.181 (leaving Canada to participate in activity of terrorist group);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 83.19(1) (facilitating terrorist activity);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.191 (leaving Canada to facilitate terrorist activity);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.2 (commission of offence for terrorist group);

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.201 (leaving Canada to commit offence for terrorist group);

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: section 83.202 (leaving Canada to commit offence that is terrorist activity);

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 83.21(1) (instructing to carry out activity for terrorist group);

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 83.22(1) (instructing to carry out terrorist activity);

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 83.221(1) (advocating or promoting commission of terrorism offences);

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 83.23(1) and (2) (concealing person who carried out terrorist activity and concealing person who is likely to carry out terrorist activity);

Paragraph.Label: (s)

Section.Text: An offence under section 42 of the Copyright Act.
An offence under any of the following provisions of the Customs Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 160(1), in respect of the contravention of any of sections 153, 155, 156, 159 and 159.1;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 160.1, in respect of the contravention of section 153.1;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 161, in respect of the contravention of section 154 or 157.

Section.Text: An offence under any of the following provisions of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 5(1) and (2) (trafficking in substance and possession for purpose of trafficking);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 6(1) and (2) (importing and exporting and possession for the purpose of exporting);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(2) (production of substance).

Section.Text: An offence under any of the following provisions of the Corruption of Foreign Public Officials Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 3(1) (bribing a foreign public official);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 4(1) (accounting);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 5(1) (offence committed outside Canada).

Section.Text: An offence under any of the following provisions of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 272(1); (contravention by persons of certain provisions);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 272.1(1); (contravention by persons of certain provisions);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 272.2 (1) (failure to comply with designated regulations);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 272.4(1), (2) and (3); (contravention by ships of certain provisions);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 272.5(1), (2) and (3); (contravention by ships of certain provisions);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 274(1) and (2) (damage to environment and risk of death or harm to persons and criminal negligence).

Section.Text: An offence under any of the following provisions of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 117(2) and (3) (organizing entry into Canada);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 118(1) (trafficking in persons);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 119 (disembarking persons at sea).

Section.Text: An offence under any of the following provisions of the Cannabis Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 9(1) and (2) (distribution and possession for purpose of distributing);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 10(1) and (2) (selling and possession for purpose of selling);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 11(1) and (2) (importing and exporting and possession for purpose of exporting);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 12(1), (4), (5), (6) and (7) (production, cultivation, propagation and harvesting);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 13(1) (possession, etc., for use in production or distribution of illicit cannabis);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 14(1) (use of young person).

Section.Text: An offence under any Act of Parliament or law of the legislature of a province that provides for the imposition of a tax or duty for which the maximum term of imprisonment is five years or more.
